title: OOF latent degradation state instead of more fixed tyre thresholds
summary: Recent F1 work models tyre energy or latent degradation as a hidden state, which is closer to the real decision variable than raw `TyreLife` or static compound life tables.
rationale: Recent F1 work models tyre energy or latent degradation as a hidden state, which is closer to the real decision variable than raw `TyreLife` or static compound life tables. Your current best solutions already exploit explicit windows heavily; a causal hidden-state summary is a more orthogonal addition.
implementation_hint: Train an out-of-fold causal teacher per driver-race-stint that predicts next-lap pace or residual tyre life from past `LapTime (s)`, `LapTime_Delta`, `TyreLife`, `Cumulative_Degradation`, `Compound`, and `RaceProgress`. Export only OOF features such as latent wear level, wear slope, and uncertainty into the main tabular model.
expected_effect: Better handling of non-linear wear and track/compound interactions, especially where the same tyre age means different pit pressure.
risk: Easy to leak future laps if the teacher is not strictly causal and race-grouped OOF.
